identity.short_name: Quadratic-probe resultant -> discriminant of a T-dependent factored polynomial

problem_spec.summary: Composite (CHAIN) family: compute bridge quantity T from component A (Engineered remainder quadratic probe resultant product); then use that T as a required parameter in component B (Discriminant mod 1000 via linear+quadratic factor chain) to determine the final output (reduced mod 1000). Instance summary: Compute a bridge coefficient T as Res(p,x^2+1) via evaluation at i, then compute Disc(F) for a linearquadraticquadratic product using the discriminant product rule and resultants, reduced mod 1000.

parameter(s).name: cubic_coefficients
parameter(s).domain: monic cubic x^3 + a x^2 + b x + c with a,b,c
parameter(s).purpose: defines the root constraint ring [x]/(P) and the multiset of roots r_i

parameter(s).name: quadratic_probe
parameter(s).domain: integer-coefficient quadratic Q(x) with deg(Q)=2 (often chosen to have conjugate roots)
parameter(s).purpose: the reduced expression whose values are multiplied over the roots of P

parameter(s).name: engineered_expression_form
parameter(s).purpose: forces the remainder of E modulo P to be exactly Q, making reduction immediate and structural

parameter(s).name: known_factorization_pattern
parameter(s).purpose: instance is designed to split as (x-r)Q1Q2 over Z

parameter(s).name: T_bridge
parameter(s).domain: derived integer computed from component A invariants
parameter(s).purpose: interface quantity produced by component A and consumed by component B (non-stapled coupling).

invariant(s).name: engineered_remainder_identity
invariant(s).statement: Since E(x)=xP(x)+Q(x), we have E(x)  Q(x) (mod P(x)), hence for every root r of P, E(r)=Q(r).

invariant(s).name: resultant_equals_product_over_roots
invariant(s).statement: For monic P, Res(P,Q)=_{P(r)=0} Q(r) (counted with multiplicity), so the target product equals a resultant.

invariant(s).name: integer_valued_symmetry
invariant(s).statement: Res(P,Q) is an integer polynomial in the coefficients of P and Q; therefore the product is an integer.

invariant(s).name: discriminant_as_resultant
invariant(s).statement: Disc(P) = (-1)^{n(n-1)/2} * Res(P, P') / a_n for deg(P)=n and leading coefficient a_n.

invariant(s).name: quadratic_discriminant_formula
invariant(s).statement: For monic quadratic x^2+bx+c, Disc = b^2 - 4c.

invariant(s).name: bridge_interface_invariant
invariant(s).statement: The composite is coupled by the interface variable T: component A uniquely determines T, and component B is defined/solved only after substituting this specific T into its parameter slot.

essential_assumption(s): Compute over C for the probe; final resultant/discriminant are integers.
Fundamental Theorem of Algebra: P has exactly three complex roots counted with multiplicity.
Work in characteristic 0 so resultants and division algorithm behave as usual.
The product over roots uses multiplicity (standard convention), matching the resultant identity.
The discriminant is taken in the standard convention via resultant with the derivative.
Modular output uses the canonical residue in {0,1,...,m-1}.
Computations are in Z[x] and Q[x], using UFD properties as needed.
The bridge quantity T computed from component A is guaranteed to be an integer in the admissible range required by component B (e.g., as an index, bound, or group-action parameter).
uniqueness_certificate(s): Resultant evaluation gives a unique integer T.
Given T, Disc(F) computed from fixed factors is unique; mod_1000 normalization is deterministic.

Because E(x)=xP(x)+Q(x), the product  E(r_i) equals  Q(r_i)=Res(P,Q). The resultant is uniquely determined by the coefficients of P and Q, hence the integer product is unique. Reducing modulo m gives a unique residue in {0,...,m-1}.

The discriminant is a deterministic integer-valued function of polynomial coefficients, and the factorization/product identities are equalities; therefore Disc(P) mod m is uniquely determined for fixed P and m.
(N1 necessity) If T is not computed from component A, component B has multiple admissible outputs across different possible parameter choices; thus the composite is ill-posed without the bridge.
(N2 non-factorization) The final answer is not a trivial Combine(ansA, ansB): the computed T restricts/changes the admissible branch structure inside component B before its output is even defined.

technique(s).name: engineered_quadratic_probe_resultant
technique(s).purpose: compute T quickly as a norm

technique(s).name: discriminant_via_factor_resultants
technique(s).purpose: assemble Disc(F) from discs and res

technique(s).name: engineered_remainder_reduction
technique(s).purpose: reduce E(r) to Q(r) instantly for all roots r of P

technique(s).name: resultant_swap_to_probe_roots
inputs: quadratic probe Q with roots α,β and leading coefficient a_Q

technique(s).name: conjugate_norm_elimination
technique(s).purpose: multiply conjugates to remove radicals/imaginary parts and obtain an integer

technique(s).name: modular_postprocessing
technique(s).purpose: report the residue class modulo m

technique(s).name: factor_by_rational_root
technique(s).purpose: split off a linear factor using the Factor Theorem

technique(s).name: factor_quartic_into_quadratics
technique(s).purpose: further factor Q into two monic quadratics to avoid a quartic discriminant formula

technique(s).name: discriminant_via_iterated_product_and_resultant
technique(s).purpose: compute Disc(P) by repeated application of Disc(AB)=Disc(A)Disc(B)Res(A,B)^2
outputs: Disc(P) in terms of quadratic discriminants and simple resultants

technique(s).name: resultant_computation_by_evaluation_at_roots
inputs: (x-r) with Q, and Q1 with Q2
technique(s).purpose: compute Res(x-r,Q)=Q(r) and compute Res(Q1,Q2) via evaluating Q2 at the roots of Q1

theorem(s).name: Polynomial Division Algorithm
theorem(s).role: guarantees remainder congruence and evaluation compatibility modulo P at roots

theorem(s).name: Resultant identity
theorem(s).role: Res(P,Q)=a_P^{deg Q} Q(r_i)=a_Q^{deg P} P(roots of Q)

theorem(s).name: Conjugate product (norm)
theorem(s).role: (u+vi)(u-vi)=u^2+v^2 (and similarly for quadratic radicals), ensuring integrality of the product

theorem(s).name: DiscriminantResultant Relation
theorem(s).role: expresses discriminant in terms of resultant with derivative

theorem(s).name: Resultant via roots (monic case)
theorem(s).role: Res(A,B)=_{α: A(α)=0} B(α) when A is monic

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Polynomial root-factor relationship and multiplicativity of discriminant via resultants.

critical_trick(s): Compute T as a complex norm using the quadratic probe x^2+1.
Feed T into a factored polynomial whose discriminant is computed by product/resultant rules.
Bridge Lemma (explicit): compute T in component A, then substitute this specific T into component B; justify N1 and N2 to rule out stapling.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: basic modular arithmetic and combinatorial counts

import_hint(s): from sympy import symbols, discriminant, factor

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: Execute the intended structural route in a CAS-friendly way: compute bridge T from component A using symbolic identities or explicit enumeration, then compute component B output with the substituted T, finally reduce mod 1000; confirm matches the recorded Answer.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Independent cross-check: avoid reusing the same derivation as primary. Use a different route (e.g., brute-force enumeration over the finite search space, numeric root approximation with direct evaluation, or an alternate invariant/Burnside count) to recompute the final answer and compare.
